author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.

classification.cautionReason: This work discusses heresy in order to refute it; do not classify it as heretical merely from its title.

lineage: Writings in Connection with the Donatist Controversy.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
In which Augustin replies to all the several statements in the letter of Petilianus, as though disputing with an adversary face to face.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Petilianus said: "If Paul uttered these words to the unlearned and to the righteous, I say this to you who are unrighteous, Is Christ divided, that you should separate yourselves from the Church?"

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: Augustin answered: I am afraid lest any one should think that in this work of mine the writer has made a mistake, and has written the heading Petilianus said, when he ought to have written Augustin answered.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But I see what your object is: you wished, as it were, to preoccupy the ground, lest we should bring those words in testimony against you.

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: But what have you really done, except to cause them to be quoted twice?

verses.book: Answer to the Letters of Petilian, the Donatist.
verses.text: If, therefore, you are so much pleased with hearing the words which make against you, as to render it necessary that they should be repeated, hear, I pray you, these words as coming from me, Petilianus: Is Christ divided, that you should separate yourselves from the Church?
